INSTALLS_YTD.title: Residential Installs YTD
INSTALLS_YTD.subtitle: Invoiced Jobs - Jan 06, 2026 - Jun 02, 2026 - De-Duplicated at Job Level - 1,571 Jobs - 14 Markets - 29 PMs

areasOfConcern: Chad Williams: 41 WOs, $449K revenue, 65.0-day median complete, top-volume PM with the slowest cycle in the network.
Multi-trade penalty is severe in 3 markets: NOVA MT 64.6d vs ST 20.7d, Raleigh MT 64.4d vs ST 21.5d, Richmond MT 49.6d vs ST 10.5d.
Days to Start averages 26.4 days company-wide and 35.1 days in Detroit Metro (a sold job sits weeks before a crew touches it).
watchList: Drew Bailey: 169 WOs, $4,995 revenue per WO, the lowest revenue density of any active high-volume PM.
Gutters-only work runs at 29.6-day median complete versus 22.7 days for roofing, 31% slower cycle on the lowest-priced trade.
Amanda Wade creates 307 jobs at $14,893 average contract and 18.2% multi-trade attach, well below the top creator.
positivesToBuildOn: May delivered $8.35M across 459 invoiced jobs at 21.7-day median complete, the highest revenue month and one of the fastest cycles of the year.
Richmond hits 16.6-day median complete and a $18,682 average contract on 79 jobs.
Multi-trade jobs carry a $25,365 average contract versus $15,766 for single-trade, a 61% revenue lift per job.
Columbus is the best-balanced market: 21.1-day median complete, 27.5% multi-trade attach, $17,193 average contract on 516 jobs.

kpis.label: Production Review
kpis.sub: 162 deals | Ops Review, PM Review, Contracted

marketJobTypeChart._description: Stacked horizontal bar; sales-by-job-type per branch.

bySubStatus.action: Follow up with insurance carrier on supplement approval. Escalate if >30 days.
Review kickback reason, correct documentation or pricing, resubmit to accounting.
No blockers, submit invoice immediately. This is free cash waiting.
Review job, identify what is blocking billing, assign owner.

fullJobList: Bernard D Young Lesa A Young
Rickey D Thomas Shirley D Thomas

whatsWorking: Sales Trajectory: Monthly sales moved from January $3.26M to June $650K (-80%). Annualized run rate: $91.76M.
Premium Deal Types: Insurance averages $20,252 per deal. Retail-Financing averages $20,969 (highest per-deal value). Retail-No Financing averages $12,070 (the volume engine).
Sold Conversion: 2,153 of 2,356 signed contracts (91.4%) have made it to Sold status for $34.27M in confirmed sales.
whatNeedsAttention: Kickback Concentration: Dayton has the most kickbacks (9, $264K). Total company kickbacks: 36 worth $759K.
Production Review Queue: 162 deals worth $3.37M sitting in Production Review. Watch for backlog growth, it delays revenue recognition.
criticalRisks: Dayton Kickback Concentration drives the company's largest single-market rework volume.
$1.66M sitting unbilled in Completed Jobs (90 jobs averaging 18 days; 3 jobs are 60+ days/$55K).
Pending Supplements aging: 57 supplement jobs ($1.11M), avg 23 days.
strengthsToAmplify: Retail Velocity: 4d median close on 1,282 retail deals.
Insurance Density: $20,252 avg on 793 deals = $16.06M; +20% lift = ~$3.21M.
June repair rate at 5.1% vs YTD 13.8%, correction in latest month.
Financing Lifts Ticket: Retail-Financing averages $20,969, highest per-deal value.
fixList: Dayton Pipeline Kickback Intervention, pull every kickback and categorize root cause.
Accounting Kickback Root Causes, 19 jobs ($281K), need a Kickback Reason field.
Production Review Bottleneck, 162 deals; add temporary PM capacity.
Financing Push, 206 financing deals YTD (8.7%) at $20,969 avg. Target 15% mix.

thisWeek: Invoice Immediately: $238K, 12 jobs marked Ready to Invoice.
Escalate 60+ Day Jobs: $55K, 3 jobs are 60+ days unbilled.
Accounting Kickback Blitz: $281K, 19 jobs kicked back; cross-functional meeting w/ accounting + sales ops.
Dayton Pipeline Kickback Review, meet with branch leadership.
Production Review Surge Plan, 162 deals ($3.37M) in queue.
thisMonth: Supplement Escalation SOP, 7/14/30 day cadence with carrier escalation.
Repair Triage Pilot in markets where repair rate exceeds 25%.
Financing Training, peer training led by top financing reps. Target 15% mix.
thisQuarter: Add Kickback Reason field to accounting workflow.
Repair Business Decision, 325 repairs YTD at ~$1,705 avg.
Ops Capacity Planning, June hit 39 deals; summer typically exceeds spring.

REVENUE_FORECAST.title: Residential Revenue Forecast
REVENUE_FORECAST.subtitle: V5 Model with Job Type Analysis · Data as of May 07, 2026

execSummary.narrative: The V5 model projects $119.6M in annual invoiced revenue against a $125.6M plan. The challenge is timing, not volume: Q1 ramped slowly so Q2 invoicing will lag. If the current weekly pace of $2.7M holds, H2 should catch up as earlier sales convert to invoiced revenue.

actionableRecommendations: Hold weekly sales pace at or above $2.7M to defend the May invoicing target.
Prioritize Retail-No Financing and Insurance work in Columbus and Detroit Metro — they carry the highest revenue-per-day.
Clear the Completed-pending-invoice queue inside seven days to keep April WIP realistic.
Audit Sold-Not-Processed dollars older than 14 days; they delay April-May conversion.
strategyHighlights: V5 cycle-time hierarchy locked: Job Type + Trade Count is the strongest predictor.
NOVA reporting now consolidated under DC Metro for cleaner branch math.
Recovery plan reallocates the YTD shortfall as a uniform uplift across May-Dec rather than a one-month spike.
WIP bridge uses the validated wip_reference.pkl baseline so April net revenue is defensible.

actionPlan.strategicGoal: Convert $1.77M of trapped partial-job revenue into billable revenue, reduce $2.16M of at-risk contract value, and clear the not-started backlog without adding headcount.
immediate: Dispatch the 42 RTS WOs sitting on partial jobs. No blocker, no hold, just dispatch.
Re-dispatch the 16 RAS WOs (oldest at 69 days). These are pure re-work fastballs.
Gutters sweep: 44 open WOs across 44 partial jobs blocking $1.24M. Highest single-trade leverage in the book.
Detroit Metro permit sweep: 69 pending-permit WOs concentrated at one branch. AHJ-relations problem, not a company-wide one.
Close out the 5 zombie jobs (all WOs Completed, parent still In Progress). Pure paperwork.
structural: Stand up a partial-job dispatch SLA: any job that crosses 14 days with at least one Completed WO and at least one open WO triggers a daily stand-up review.
Add a Permit Aging escalation path: any pending-permit WO over 14 days routes to the branch GM with a daily AHJ touchpoint requirement.
Trade-specific dispatch surge for the dominant trailing trade (currently Gutters): evaluate whether sub-fleet expansion or schedule re-balance moves the number faster than headcount.
Pending Sales disposition cadence: weekly meeting with the top stuck reps to triage. Most are dispositions, not deals to lose.
Not-Started intake review: 476 jobs ($9.73M) sit waiting. Audit the dispatch trigger so jobs do not languish post-signature.
cadence: Weekly Monday Action Plan refresh: re-baseline the Immediate list every 7 days.
Daily branch standup includes the Permit Aging report and any RAS WO over 30 days.
Bi-weekly partial-job review: walk the trailing-trades table with the production scheduler.
Monthly Salesperson View read: surface the top stuck reps to sales leadership for joint disposition.
Quarterly Trade Analysis read: validate that Roofing-to-Gutters cadence still matches install volume.
actionPlan.bottomLine: The book is healthy in volume terms. The drag is in the middle of the funnel: partial jobs trap $1.77M, holds are concentrated in permits, and the not-started cohort needs an intake audit. The fix list is operational, not strategic. The top three workstreams (RTS dispatch, RAS re-dispatch, permit sweep) move the number without adding headcount.
